symptoms.symptom_name: Adherent white curd-like plaques over tongue and buccal mucosa

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Adherent curd-like pseudomembranous plaques are the hallmark of pseudomembranous oral candidiasis.
Curd-like removable plaques of acute onset are unlike the fixed, non-removable white patches of leukoplakia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cottony mouth sensation with loss of taste (dysgeusia)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A cottony mouth sensation and dysgeusia are common accompaniments of oral candidiasis coating the tongue.

symptoms.symptom_name: Localised oral burning pain aggravated by acidic, spicy or rough foods

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The friable erythematous mucosal base under the plaques produces localised burning aggravated by irritant foods.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent 7-day broad-spectrum antibiotic course immediately preceding onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Broad-spectrum antibiotic exposure disrupts commensal flora and is a leading precipitant of oral candidiasis; the tight temporal link is highly suggestive.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral angular cheilitis at the oral commissures

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Angular cheilitis frequently co-occurs with oral candidiasis and is often candidal in origin; visible on video.

symptoms.symptom_name: Plaques removable on scraping revealing an erythematous friable base

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Plaques that rub off to reveal an erythematous friable base are the classic bedside discriminator confirming pseudomembranous candidiasis over leukoplakia — a clinician manoeuvre unavailable in telehealth chat.
Leukoplakic lesions are fixed and do not rub off; removability excludes leukoplakia.

symptoms.symptom_name: KOH preparation of mucosal scraping showing budding yeasts and pseudohyphae

investigation_details.investigation_name: KOH microscopy of oral mucosal scraping
investigation_details.expected_result: Budding yeasts and pseudohyphae consistent with Candida — confirmatory but not required when the clinical picture is classic
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: None for management — the diagnosis is clinical in a classic post-antibiotic presentation; KOH is reserved for atypical or refractory cases.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Yeasts with pseudohyphae on KOH confirm candidiasis; a laboratory step, not obtainable in a telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of deep retrosternal pain on swallowing (no odynophagia)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep retrosternal odynophagia is the cardinal symptom of oesophageal extension; its absence is required to keep the disease in the mild oral category.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile with normal clinician-measured vital signs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal vitals and no fever are consistent with mild localised candidiasis and argue against systemic infection; the objective clinician measurement is sealed — the AI Doctor relies on the patient-reported home temperature only.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced oral intake with moderate dehydration risk, compounded by living alone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Not diagnostic of candidiasis, but the pain-driven fluid restriction is the case's main acuity driver and the reason it warrants prompt attention and safety-netting.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Recent broad-spectrum antibiotics + adherent curd-like plaques + cottony mouth/loss of taste + angular cheilitis together constitute a high-probability oral candidiasis picture recognisable from history and video without an in-person exam.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: KOH microscopy of oral mucosal scraping
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not required when the presentation is classic post-antibiotic pseudomembranous candidiasis; reserved for atypical, refractory or diagnostically uncertain cases. Not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Random blood glucose / HbA1c if recurrent or persistent
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Only if candidiasis is recurrent or persistent — to screen for an underlying predisposing condition such as undiagnosed diabetes; not indicated for this first, antibiotic-explained episode.
